steps: Transfer 20 mg - 50 mg frozen brain tissue to microcentrifuge tube containing 500 µL of NIM. Transfer the tissue in a tube with the lid on using a pair of forceps.

Gently triturate the tissue with a cut pipette tip ( 1 mL tip). Repeat several times with pipette tips cut less (estimate 3-4 different cut sizes). Tissue will be perfectly dissociated when able to pass through an uncut pipette tip.
Measure the total volume using 1 mL pipette.
Add Triton X-100 solution to the sample to achieve final concentration 0.1% (stock solution 10%).

Homogenize using 2 mL Dounce tissue grinders: a. Grinder A (loose, large clearance pestles): 2-3 strokes. b. Grinder B (tight, small clearance pestles): 8-12 strokes. Note: The sample solution should appear homogeneous at this point, but the use of the Dounce grinders allow nuclei to remain intact.
Transfer the homogenate to a fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, , 00:08:00 , at 4 °C - Discard Supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet gently in 700 µL 25% Iodixanol Solution.

Layer the sample (avoiding mixing the phases) onto 700 µL 29% iodixanol solution. Use 1 mL syringe without needle to slowly load and suspend the sample on top. As a result, 2 clearly defined phases should appear as follows: Figure 2: Nuclear isolation using iodixanol gradient. A. Gradient generation solution is generated from 25% iodixanol gradient containing the cell lysate on the top and 29% iodixanol gradient solution on the bottom. B. After centrifugation, the nuclear fraction is near the bottom of the tube.
Spin at 10300 x g, , , 00:20:00 , at 4 °C - remove the supernatant leaving 50 µL in the bottom of the ultracentrifuge tube (contain the nuclear fraction).
At this point, follow any of the below routes:

Note: Although we routinely use the nuclear suspensions on the day of preparation, others have reported those to be stable at at 4 °C for several weeks when stored in nuclei storage buffer (Reed et al ., 2017).
Resuspend nuclei in DAPI (final 1 µg/ml ) diluted in 1000 µL ice-cold PBS.
Incubate for 00:20:00 at 4 °C (or 01:00:00 at 4 °C ).
Immediately after isolation, resuspend the pelleted nuclei in 500 µL of pre-chilled Blocking buffer.

Use a haemocytometer to estimate the number of nuclei in the final suspension. In a good nuclear fraction, most of the nuclei should be seen as single, with few clumps and/or low amount of cellular/tissue debris.
Spin at 800 x g, °C, , for 00:10:00 , at 4 °C - Remove the supernatant.
Resuspend nuclear pellet in 500 µL ice-cold PBS.
Check nuclei under the microscope using a hemacytometer.

Note: Use a haemocytometer to estimate the number of nuclei in the final suspension. In a good nuclear fraction, most of the nuclei should be seen as single, with few clumps and/or low amount of cellular/tissue debris.
Incubate at 4 °C for 00:30:00 with gentle shaking on a rotator disk to block non-specific binding sites.

Add primary antibody at the optimal concentration directly to the blocking buffer containing the nuclear suspension and incubate at 4 °C for 01:00:00 with gentle shaking. Note: The optimal working concentration for each antibody needs to be determined in advance by titration assays.

Pellet nuclei by centrifugation at 800 x g, , at 4 °C for 00:10:00 , remove supernatant taking care not to disturb the pellet, and gently resuspend it in pre-chilled PBS to wash off excess primary antibody.
Pellet again the nuclei as stated above, discard supernatant.
Add 2 µg/ml goat anti-mouse and/or anti-rabbit secondary antibody and 1 µg/ml DAPI diluted in Blocking buffer.
Incubate for 01:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking on a rotator disk.
After incubation, wash the excess of PBS by pelleting again the nuclei and resuspending them in fresh PBS/PIC.
Critical note : If possible, do sub-sections 1 and 2 in a PCR cabinet to minimize contamination.

Visually inspect the CytoSort array prior opening. The pre-sterilized arrays are expected to have a red sticker on packaging. Remove the array from the sealed pouch. Figure 3 . Examples of CytoSort arrays prior to opening (A) without, and (B) with crystallization.
Wash with 2 mL dH2O – incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature – Remove water.
Repeat the washing with 2 mL dH2O – incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature - Remove water.
Apply 1 mL of 15 ug/mL Cell-Tak in PBS with 2.5 millimolar (mM) NaOH – incubate for 00:20:00 @ Room temperature – Remove Cell-Talk.
Add 2 mL sterile PBS 1x - Incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature – Remove PBS.
Repeat step 21 (3 times in total):
Optional step: Insert the CellRaft seeding insert to increase the overall number of single cells available for isolation.

Dilute approximately 5000 nuclei in 2 mL of PBS, add pipette them dropwise in the centre of the CytoSort Array. Critical note : Nuclei should be counted in a haemocytometer prior to seeding. The number of nuclei seeded on the array is a key factor, as too many will lead to few CellRafts with single nuclei, while too little will lead to most of the CellRafts being empty, according to a Poisson distribution. Thus, seeding the array with a nuclei: wells ratio 1:2 or 1:3 increases the proportion of wells capturing a single nucleus versus wells capturing none or >2 nuclei.
Move the CytoSort Array carefully to ensure the nuclei settle evenly within the array (like seeding cells for cell culture).

Allow it to settle for 02:00:00, - O/N in the fridge. Note 1 : Use parafilm, paper towel and foil to properly seal the array to prevent desiccation Note 2 : From our experience, nuclei are stable on the array for up to 336:00:00 when stored properly sealed in the fridge.

View CytoSort Arrays under the 10x magnification objective with appropriate fluorescence filters, to select rafts (microwells) containing a single nucleus with the desired characteristics and take photos (if needed Fig 4). Figure 4. Manual picking of rafts containing the nuclei of interest. A CellRaft with a single nucleus stained by DAPI.
Individually release the arrays of interest (Figure 5). Figure 5 . Example of released and removed CellRafts.

Place the magnetic wand in the tubes containing DNase-I diluted solution. Critical note: DNAse-I solution is used to reduce cross-contamination between samples. This may require optimisation in each lab.
Wash the wand with 70% EtOH and then with dH2O.
Capture, release and remove the released raft to the already prepared 0.2 mL tubes with the buffer needed for scWGA using the magnetic want. Then, centrifuge the tubes briefly.

When performing CellRaft isolation, it is highly recommended to ensure the rafts are properly released at the bottom of the tube and not in the tube wall, as they would desiccate. To do so, visually inspect every tube after collection under a stereoscopic microscope; rafts will appear as small orange squares. Critical note : As controls, use at least one of each of the following per experiment: Negative control 1: a tube with no raft, in which the magnetic wand has been dipped after it was dipped into the array covering fluid. Negative control 2: a raft with no nucleus. Positive control: 15 pg of genomic DNA. Figure 6 . Wand washing, raft picking and release in 0.2 ml tubes.

Freeze the samples at -80 °C or proceed directly to scWGA. For scWGA we have used the following methodologies: PicoPlex (R300670, R300672 and R300722) according to Takara’s protocols. dMDA with heat denaturation and Buffer Generation (dMDA protocol attached). PTA (PTA protocol attached) but for step 7: incubate for 00:20:00 at Room temperature . Note: So far, we have successfully used the PicoPlex method with and without nuclei immunostaining. However, we have not tested the dMDA and PTA methods in combination with immunostaining, but we have just stained the nuclei with DAPI staining.
Critical note : If possible, do sub-sections 1 and 2 in a PCR cabinet to minimize contamination.

Visually inspect the CytoSort array prior opening. The pre-sterilized arrays are expected to have a red sticker on packaging. Remove the array from the sealed pouch. Figure 3 . Examples of CytoSort arrays prior to opening (A) without, and (B) with crystallization.
Wash with 2 mL dH2O – incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature – Remove water.
Repeat the washing with 2 mL dH2O – incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature - Remove water.
Apply 1 mL of 15 µg/ml Cell-Tak in PBS with 2.5 millimolar (mM) NaOH – incubate for 00:20:00 at Room temperature – Remove Cell-Talk.
Add 2 mL sterile PBS 1x - Incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature – Remove PBS.
Repeat step 25 (3 times in total).
Optional step: Insert the CellRaft seeding insert to increase the overall number of single cells available for isolation.

Dilute approximately 5000 nuclei in 2 mL of PBS, add pipette them dropwise in the centre of the CytoSort Array. Critical note : Nuclei should be counted in a haemocytometer prior to seeding. The number of nuclei seeded on the array is a key factor, as too many will lead to few CellRafts with single nuclei, while too little will lead to most of the CellRafts being empty, according to a Poisson distribution.
